title: Eria retusa
taxonomy: Eria retusa is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae, which is one of the largest and most diverse plant families. This family includes over 30,000 known species, with Eria retusa being just one of the many unique and fascinating species within it. The Orchidaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Eria retusa falling under the subfamily Epidendroideae. The exact classification of Eria retusa can be broken down further into its genus and species levels, with its genus name indicating its close evolutionary relationship to other Eria species. By understanding the classification of Eria retusa, we can gain a deeper appreciation for the intricate and complex relationships within the plant kingdom.

parts: Eria retusa, also known as the "clubbed orchid," is a plant species with various medicinal properties. intro: Eria retusa is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia, often used in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Eria retusa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
